asks: The distance between two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) is given by the formula \sqrt{(x2 - x1)^2 + (y2 - y1)^2}. The vertical difference is y2 - y1, and the horizontal difference is x2 - x1. The image illustrates the calculation of the Euclidean distance between two points in a coordinate plane using their coordinates. distance between two points in coordinate system coordinates distance formula √((x2 - x1)^2 + (y2 - y1)^2) \sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2}
topic: distance between two points in coordinate system
